Jonathan Joestar is the first JoJo and a true gentleman.
Dio Brando grew up in poverty before becoming a vampire.
Jonathan Joestar dies on a ship with Dio at the end of Part 1.
Will A. Zeppeli sacrifices himself to save Jonathan.
Hamon is a breathing technique that mimics sunlight.
Speedwagon starts as a thug but becomes a loyal ally.
Joseph Joestar is known for outsmarting enemies.
Joseph often predicts what his opponent will say next.
Caesar Zeppeli dies fighting the Pillar Men.
Kars becomes the ultimate life form.
Stands are first introduced in Stardust Crusaders.
Jotaro Kujo’s Stand is Star Platinum.
Star Platinum has incredible speed and precision.
Dio Brando returns as DIO in Part 3.
Jotaro eventually learns to stop time as well.
Iggy is a dog with a Stand.
The Stardust Crusaders travel to Egypt.
Koichi Hirose’s Stand evolves three times.
Yoshikage Kira is a serial killer.
Killer Queen can turn anything into a bomb.
Jotaro appears in Part 4 as an adult.
Diavolo is the main villain of Part 5.
Part 6 takes place in a prison.
Enrico Pucci is the main villain.
The universe resets at the end of Part 6.
Steel Ball Run is set in an alternate universe.
Johnny Joestar is a former jockey.
Johnny is paralyzed at the start.
Gyro Zeppeli teaches Johnny the Spin.
The Spin is a technique using rotation.
Gyro uses steel balls as weapons.
The Steel Ball Run is a cross-country race.
Valentine’s Stand is Dirty Deeds Done Dirt Cheap.
Gyro dies during the final battle.
The Saint’s Corpse is a key plot element.
Lucy Steel plays a major role.
Diego Brando is an alternate version of Dio.
Diego uses the Stand Scary Monsters.
Josuke in Part 8 is a fusion of two people.
The Higashikata family is central in Part 8.
Yasuho helps Josuke throughout the story.
The Rokakaka fruit has special powers.
Many Stands are named after music.
The series mixes horror and humor.
Each JoJo has a unique personality.
King Crimson’s ability is confusing to many fans.
Johnny slowly learns to walk again.
Valentine believes he is helping America.
Spin is tied to the Golden Ratio.
Johnny rides a horse in battles.
Steel Ball Run includes many racers.
Ringo Roadagain believes in fair duels.
Blackmore uses rain as a weapon.
Diego returns later in the story.
Johnny sacrifices a lot to win.
Each part has a different theme.
Power is not everything in JoJo.
Araki is inspired by Western culture.
DIO is one of the most iconic villains.
Kira is more grounded and realistic.
Johnny is one of the darkest JoJos.
Steel Ball Run is highly praised.
Gold Experience Requiem is nearly unbeatable.
The ending of SBR is bittersweet.
JoJo continues to evolve as a series
